name: Prompt Cleanup

tasks.title: Remove Phase H and git instructions from execute.md
tasks.description: Remove Phase H and git instructions from execute.md
tasks.body:
Edit .wolfcastle/system/base/prompts/stages/execute.md to:

1. Remove Phase H (Commit) entirely (lines 151-158). This section instructs the agent to run git add -u, git add .wolfcastle/, and git commit.
2. Search for and remove any other git-related instructions in the file (e.g., the git add .wolfcastle/ instruction added in PR #137).
3. Renumber remaining phases if Phase H was between other lettered phases.
4. Verify the terminal markers (WOLFCASTLE_COMPLETE, etc.) remain intact. The agent still signals completion; it just doesn't commit.

After editing, grep the file for 'git add', 'git commit', 'Phase H' to verify nothing remains.
acceptance_criteria: or Phase H in the file
Terminal markers still present and documented

tasks.title: Remove git instructions from agent-facing docs
tasks.description: Remove git instructions from agent-facing docs
tasks.body:
Review and update all agent-facing documentation to remove any guidance telling agents to run git commands:

Files to review:
- AGENTS.md (repository root)
- docs/agents/README.md
- docs/agents/daemon.md
- docs/agents/commands.md
- docs/agents/code-standards.md
- docs/agents/architecture.md
- docs/agents/state-and-types.md
- All other files in docs/agents/

For each file:
1. Remove any instructions telling agents to run git add, git commit, or other git commands
2. Add a brief note where appropriate that agents should NOT run git commands (the daemon handles all git operations)
3. Do not over-add warnings; one clear statement per file that mentions git is sufficient

Grep all files in docs/agents/ and AGENTS.md for 'git add', 'git commit', 'git push', 'git checkout' to find all references.
acceptance_criteria: No agent-facing doc instructs agents to run git commands
At least one clear statement exists that agents should not run git commands

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Prompt Cleanup is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Grep execute.md for git add, git commit, Phase H and confirm zero matches
Grep docs/agents/ and AGENTS.md for instructions to run git commands

scope.description: Verify removal of git instructions from agent-facing prompts and documentation
criteria: No instructional git references in agent docs

breadcrumbs.text: Removed Phase H (Commit), all git add/commit instructions, and rewrote Boundaries to prohibit agent git usage. Renumbered phases G-I. Updated rules and WOLFCASTLE_COMPLETE docs.
Removed git instructions from AGENTS.md, AUDIT.md, and code-standards.md. Replaced with daemon-owns-git guidance. All agent docs now clean of git command instructions.

Audit reviewed 4 files (~250 lines of changes across execute.md, AGENTS.md, AUDIT.md, code-standards.md). PASS. Phase lettering in execute.md is contiguous A-I with no gaps. All instructional git references removed from agent docs; only prohibitive references remain (Do NOT lists). Descriptive git references (package names, daemon architecture) correctly preserved. Build clean, all tests pass. One escalation: pre-existing gofmt violations in daemon test files outside this node's scope. Quality: clean, surgical edits. Both tasks identified the correct boundary between instructional and descriptive git references and acted accordingly.

audit.result_summary: Removed all instructional git references from agent-facing prompts and documentation, establishing the daemon as the sole owner of git operations. Task 1 rewrote execute.md: removed the Commit phase, renumbered phases A-I for contiguity, rewrote Boundaries to prohibit agent git usage, and updated rules and terminal marker docs. Task 2 updated AGENTS.md, AUDIT.md, and code-standards.md to replace git command instructions with daemon-owns-git guidance while preserving descriptive references to git packages and daemon architecture. Audit confirmed all changes are correct, build and tests pass, and no instructional git references remain in agent-facing docs.

audit.objective: Verify all work in the prompt-cleanup node is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Read all four modified files (execute.md, AGENTS.md, AUDIT.md, code-standards.md). Grepped for remaining instructional git references, finding none outside of prohibitive Do NOT lists. Verified phase lettering A-I is contiguous. Ran build, tests (all pass), and lint (gofmt issue in daemon test files is pre-existing).
went_well: Both tasks correctly distinguished instructional from descriptive git references
Phase renumbering in execute.md is clean with no gaps or orphaned references
improvements: None identified; the edits were surgical and well-scoped
action_items: docs/humans/ files still reference the old agent-commits model per task-0002 AAR; tracked as separate work outside this node

task-0001.objective: Remove git instructions from execute.md so the daemon owns all git operations
task-0001.what_happened: Edited .wolfcastle/system/base/prompts/stages/execute.md: removed Phase H (Commit) entirely, renumbered phases F→G→H→I for contiguity, rewrote Boundaries section to prohibit git commands instead of directing where to commit, removed 'Git progress is the hard gate' line, updated WOLFCASTLE_COMPLETE description to reference daemon committing, replaced 'Commit before signaling completion' rule with 'Never run git commands' rule.
went_well: The only remaining git add/commit mention is in the Do NOT list, which is correct
Phase lettering is now contiguous A through I with no gaps
improvements: The Boundaries section still mentions worktrees; once the daemon fully owns branch management, that guidance may need a second pass
action_items: Downstream docs (AGENTS.md, how-it-works.md) still reference agent commits and need updating per the spec

task-0002.objective: Remove git instructions from agent-facing documentation
task-0002.what_happened: Edited four files: AGENTS.md rule 6 replaced git pull/rebase instruction with a blanket 'do not run git commands' rule. AUDIT.md line 21 changed 'refuses to commit' to 'refuses to proceed'. AUDIT.md rules 3-4 collapsed into one rule removing 'commit as you go' instruction. code-standards.md changed 'before every commit' to 'before signaling completion'. Left descriptive git references intact (architecture package names, test config helpers, documentation.md commit-message context).
went_well: Grep confirmed zero instructional git references remain in agent docs
Descriptive references to the git package and daemon git config were correctly preserved
improvements: The daemon.md file didn't need changes since it already described git as a daemon responsibility, not an agent one
action_items: docs/humans/ files still reference the old agent-commits model and need updating per the spec (separate task)
